title: EtherChannel with LACP
description: Configure a Layer 2 EtherChannel between two switches using LACP, verify the bundle, and troubleshoot common issues.
instructions:
## EtherChannel with LACP Lab

Configure a 2-link EtherChannel between two switches using LACP and set it up as a trunk.

### Topology

```
         Gi0/1 -------- Gi0/1
[SW1]                          [SW2]
         Gi0/2 -------- Gi0/2
```

- Both links should be bundled into Port-channel 1
- The EtherChannel should operate as an 802.1Q trunk
- VLANs 10, 20, and 30 should be allowed on the trunk
- Native VLAN should be set to 99

### Requirements

#### SW1 (LACP Active):
1. Configure Gi0/1 and Gi0/2 as LACP **active** members of channel-group 1
2. Configure Port-channel 1 as a trunk with native VLAN 99
3. Restrict the trunk to VLANs 10, 20, 30, and 99

#### SW2 (LACP Passive):
4. Configure Gi0/1 and Gi0/2 as LACP **passive** members of channel-group 1
5. Configure Port-channel 1 with identical trunk settings as SW1

#### Verification:
6. What commands verify the EtherChannel status and member ports?
starterCode:
! ========== SWITCH SW1 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 1: Configure member interfaces
!
!
! Step 2-3: Configure Port-channel trunk
!
!
end
!
! ========== SWITCH SW2 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 4: Configure member interfaces
!
!
! Step 5: Configure Port-channel trunk
!
!
end
!
! Step 6: Verification
!
solutionCode:
! ========== SWITCH SW1 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
interface range GigabitEthernet0/1-2
 channel-group 1 mode active
 no shutdown
!
interface Port-channel1
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30,99
 no shutdown
!
end
!
! ========== SWITCH SW2 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
interface range GigabitEthernet0/1-2
 channel-group 1 mode passive
 no shutdown
!
interface Port-channel1
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30,99
 no shutdown
!
end
!
! Verification:
show etherchannel summary
show etherchannel port-channel
show interfaces trunk
show lacp neighbor
expectedOutput:
SW1#show etherchannel summary
Flags:  D - down        P - bundled in port-channel
        I - stand-alone s - suspended
        H - Hot-standby (LACP only)
        R - Layer3      S - Layer2
        U - in use

Group  Port-channel  Protocol    Ports
------+-------------+-----------+-------------------
1      Po1(SU)         LACP      Gi0/1(P) Gi0/2(P)

SW1#show interfaces trunk
Port        Mode         Encapsulation  Status        Native vlan
Po1         on           802.1q         trunking      99

VLANs allowed on trunk: 10,20,30,99
hints: Configure the channel-group on the physical interfaces FIRST, then configure trunk settings on the Port-channel interface. Settings applied to the Port-channel are inherited by member ports.
LACP active-passive will form a channel (active initiates negotiation). LACP passive-passive will NOT form because neither side initiates.
All member ports must have identical settings (speed, duplex, VLAN, trunk mode). If they don't match, ports will be suspended. Use 'show etherchannel summary' to check — look for (P) meaning bundled.
learningObjectives: Configure LACP EtherChannel with active and passive modes
Apply trunk configuration to the Port-channel logical interface
Understand LACP negotiation modes and valid combinations
Verify EtherChannel formation and troubleshoot common issues
